name: Chapter 1: Waiting for Automation (1960-1969)
description: Peter S. Kastner recounts his formative years growing up on Cape Cod running the bookkeeping department of his family's large restaurant, where the labor-intensive paper reconciliation problem planted the seed for a lifelong drive to automate commerce. He traces his path from Cornell University's Hotel School — where a failed punch-card tabulation experiment and an IBM 360/65 operations job deepened his technical grounding — through a year at MIT's computing center, where he encountered timesharing, early AI, and the first DEC minicomputers. The chapter closes with his contributions to Robert Chase's hotel-management simulation software at Cornell and his departure for Philip Hankins Inc. in Boston, still in pursuit of the paper problem that had never been solved.
